mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS signals easier discounting and lower financing stress, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, higher policy rates tighten financial conditions, raise the equity discount rate, and predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:policy rate regime above neutral can compress equity multiples
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level indicates deteriorating labor demand and weaker household income growth, which lowers expected sales and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for the same-month trend in PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deterioration in PAYEMS and UNRATE captures a true labor-market break: payroll losses plus rising unemployment reduce earnings expectations and should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction does not outperform either series alone, or if negative 60-day returns are not concentrated in months with payroll contraction and rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, which improves earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher RSAFS growth is followed by below-zero 20-day returns on average, or if the signal loses sign consistency in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO after a prior peak signals weakening industrial production and slower real activity, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if sub-trend INDPRO readings fail to precede below-zero 60-day returns, or if the average return after triggers is not lower than after non-trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls while RSAFS also weakens, housing demand and broad consumption are both softening, which signals a demand rollover that should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint housing-sales weakness signal does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered months do not show a lower mean 20-day return than the sample baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising GDPC1 combined with falling UNRATE reflects improving output and labor absorption, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP-up/ unemployment-down periods do not lead to positive 60-day returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no predictive power beyond GDPC1 alone.
